ddls:
CREATE TABLE "access_points" (
  "ap_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access point */,
  "ssid" TEXT /* Service Set Identifier (SSID) of the AP */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the AP */,
  "phy_type" TEXT /* Physical type of the AP (e.g., 802.11n) */,
  "frequency" REAL /* Frequency band used by the AP */,
  "channel" INTEGER /* Channel number used by the AP */,
  "information_size" INTEGER /* Size of the information element in the beacon frame */,
  "elements_count" INTEGER /* Count of elements in the beacon frame */,
  "company" TEXT /* Company that manufactures the AP */,
  "router_model" TEXT /* Model of the router */,
  "router_name" TEXT /* Name of the router */,
  "bss_type" TEXT /* Basic Service Set (BSS) type */,
  "wps_support" TEXT /* Whether Wi-Fi Protected Setup (WPS) is supported */,
  "first_detection" TEXT /* Timestamp of the first detection of the AP */,
  "last_detection" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last detection of the AP */,
  "detection_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the AP has been detected */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the start of monitoring */,
  "min_signal_quality" INTEGER /* Minimum signal quality recorded */,
  "max_signal_quality" INTEGER /* Maximum signal quality recorded */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the AP's physical location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the AP's physical location */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Version of the firmware running on the AP */,
  "uptime" INTEGER /* Uptime of the AP in seconds */,
  "last_reboot" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last reboot of the AP */,
  "description" TEXT /* Additional description or notes about the AP */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the AP (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ap_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client device */,
  "ap_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access point the client is connected to */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the client device */,
  "first_connection" TEXT /* Timestamp of the first connection to the AP */,
  "last_connection" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last connection to the AP */,
  "connection_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the client has connected to the AP */,
  "client_type" TEXT /* Type of client device (e.g., smartphone, laptop, IoT device) */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system of the client device */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the client device */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the last session in seconds */,
  "data_usage" INTEGER /* Data usage in bytes during the last session */,
  "last_ip_address" TEXT /* Last IP address assigned to the client device */,
  "last_signal_quality" INTEGER /* Last recorded signal quality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clients_ap_id FOREIGN KEY ("ap_id") REFERENCES access_points ("ap_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric record */,
  "ap_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access point */,
  "rssi" INTEGER /* Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) */,
  "signal_quality" INTEGER /* Current signal quality */,
  "average_signal_quality" INTEGER /* Average signal quality over time */,
  "max_speed" TEXT /* Maximum speed supported by the AP */,
  "channel_width" TEXT /* Channel width used by the AP */,
  "channels_range" TEXT /* Range of channels used by the AP */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  "packet_loss" REAL /* Percentage of packet loss */,
  "jitter" REAL /* Jitter in milliseconds */,
  "latency" REAL /* Latency in milliseconds */,
  "throughput" INTEGER /* Throughput in bytes per second */,
  "retransmissions" INTEGER /* Number of retransmissions */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate in percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_ap_id FOREIGN KEY ("ap_id") REFERENCES access_points ("ap_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security policy record */,
  "ap_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access point */,
  "security_type" TEXT /* Type of security used (e.g., WPA-PSK + WPA2-PSK) */,
  "cipher" TEXT /* Cipher used for encryption (e.g., TKIP+CCMP) */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the security policy */,
  "psk" TEXT /* Pre-shared key (PSK) for WPA-PSK and WPA2-PSK */,
  "encryption" TEXT /* Type of encryption (e.g., AES, TKIP) */,
  "authentication" TEXT /* Authentication method (e.g., WPA-PSK, WPA2-PSK) */,
  "security_status" TEXT /* Current status of the security policy (e.g., compliant, non-compliant) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_policies_ap_id FOREIGN KEY ("ap_id") REFERENCES access_points ("ap_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "ap_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access point */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., office, warehouse) */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environmental context (e.g., indoor, outdoor) */,
  "deployment_type" TEXT /* Type of deployment (e.g., infrastructure, ad-hoc) */,
  "building" TEXT /* Name of the building where the AP is located */,
  "floor" TEXT /* Floor number where the AP is located */,
  "room" TEXT /* Room number where the AP is located */,
  "site" TEXT /* Site or campus where the AP is located */,
  "network_segment" TEXT /* Network segment or subnet where the AP is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_ap_id FOREIGN KEY ("ap_id") REFERENCES access_points ("ap_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, network engineer, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity */,
  "session_token" TEXT /* Token for the current session */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON string of user permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
